title: [TASK] Set up Docker Compose for local development orchestration

conversation_log.summary: Task is fully consistent with architecture.md and ADR-004. File locations (docker/docker-compose.yml, docker/Dockerfile.frontend, docker/Dockerfile.backend) match the canonical folder structure. Service definitions (frontend, backend, db) align with the architecture overview. Environment variable naming follows SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE convention. .env.example placement at repo root matches the environment configuration pattern. Dev-only Dockerfiles respect the local-development-only constraint. Volume mounts reference correct monorepo paths (apps/frontend, apps/backend). Only backend connects to PostgreSQL, preserving the access pattern rule.
Requirements Specialist marked this issue as a duplicate of #10. Canonical task with full conversation history is #10. All sign-off activity to continue on #10.
Tester acknowledged duplicate status. No testability review performed on #17. All acceptance criteria review activity is on canonical task issue #10. No further action on this issue.

Implemented Docker Compose local development orchestration. Created docker/docker-compose.yml (3 services: frontend, backend, db; user-defined network cv-tool-network; named volume postgres_data; bind-mounts for hot-reload; db healthcheck; backend depends_on db healthy). Created docker/Dockerfile.frontend and docker/Dockerfile.backend (development-only, node:20-alpine, no multi-stage/production build, curl installed for connectivity verification). Created .env.example at repo root with all 6 required keys (POSTGRES_USER, POSTGRES_PASSWORD, POSTGRES_DB, BACKEND_PORT, FRONTEND_PORT, DATABASE_URL). Created .gitignore ensuring .env is excluded. Created README.md with Local Development section (clone -> copy .env.example -> docker compose up from docker/). No hardcoded secrets in any tracked file. PR #18 opened.

decisions.approach: Created docker/docker-compose.yml with three services (frontend, backend, db) connected via a user-defined bridge network. PostgreSQL data persisted via named volume. Bind-mounts for apps/frontend and apps/backend enable hot-reload. node_modules anonymous volume override prevents host bind-mount from shadowing container-installed dependencies. db healthcheck with pg_isready ensures backend only starts once PostgreSQL is ready. curl installed in both Dockerfiles for in-container connectivity verification (AC #7). All sensitive values sourced from .env via env_file directive — no hardcoded secrets.
risks: apps/frontend and apps/backend directories must exist (from monorepo setup task) before docker compose up is fully functional — bind-mounts will create empty directories otherwise.
Dockerfiles COPY root package.json, turbo.json, tsconfig.json, and workspace package.json files at build time — these must exist (from monorepo setup task) before images can be built.

Issue #17 ACs consistently use 'db' as the service name. Issue #10's Architect review requested 'postgres' as the service name. Implemented as 'db' per issue #17's ACs since that is the assigned task. This may need reconciliation with issue #10.
